engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Clio Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Clio in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Clio K9K engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector wear - high-pressure injectors degrade from 80k miles, causing rough idle and misfires.
Turbo failure - variable geometry vanes stick on high-mileage units, triggering limp mode.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow, causing power loss and increased emissions.

cards.altText: Renault Clio D4F engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Dephaser pulley noise - VVT unit wears, causing rattling on cold start and timing issues.
Coil pack failure - ignition coils fail prematurely, leading to misfires and rough running.
Valve stem seal wear - oil consumption increases after 70k miles on higher-mileage units.

cards.altText: Renault Clio H5F engine - petrol 1.2L turbo replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - chain tensioner fails, causing chain stretch and potential engine damage from 60k miles.
Carbon buildup on intake valves - direct injection design leads to performance loss without walnut blasting.
High-pressure fuel pump failure - pump wear causes hard starting and loss of power under load.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £850-£1,900 (supply only) Estimated from comparable H5F units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Clio Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Clio across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: The K9K 1.5 dCi became the Clio's diesel backbone, offering strong economy but known for injector and turbo wear on high-mileage units.
compatibleVariants: Expression, Dynamique, GT Line, Privilege, Initiale, 1.5 dCi 65, 1.5 dCi 75, 1.5 dCi 85, 1.5 dCi 90, 1.5 dCi 110
commonFailures: High-pressure injector wear - causes rough idle and misfires from 80k miles
Turbo VGT actuator failure - sticks, triggering limp mode and power loss
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow, increasing emissions
entries.cta: Get quotes for K9K Renault Clio engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The D4F 1.2 16v powered entry-level Clios for over a decade, valued for simplicity but prone to dephaser and ignition issues.
commonFailures: Dephaser pulley wear - causes rattling on cold start and timing issues
Ignition coil failure - leads to misfires and rough running
Valve stem seal wear - increases oil consumption after 70k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4F Renault Clio engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: The H5F 1.2 TCe introduced downsized turbo power to the Clio, but timing chain stretch remains a well-documented failure point.
compatibleVariants: Dynamique, GT Line, Privilege, 1.2 TCe 90, 1.2 TCe 120, GT
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch - tensioner failure causes chain wear from 60k miles
Carbon buildup on intake valves - reduces performance without walnut blasting
High-pressure fuel pump wear - causes hard starting and power loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for H5F Renault Clio engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The F4R powered the iconic Renaultsport 197 and 200, delivering thrilling performance but requiring careful cambelt maintenance.
commonFailures: Cambelt failure risk - if not changed at 6-year/72k mile intervals
Oil consumption on high-mileage units - piston ring wear
Clutch and flywear on RS models - driven hard from new
entries.cta: Get quotes for F4R Renault Clio engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Renault Clio Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Clio's most common engine issues centre on the 1.5 dCi diesel and 1.2 TCe petrol families. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Clio.

problems.h4: Renault Clio 1.5 dCi Injector Failure - Why K9K Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: High-pressure piezo injectors degrade internally, causing poor atomisation, rough idle, and increased fuel consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, bench-test and clean injectors; replace seals if viable
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; only viable before complete injector failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injectors with reconditioned units; code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: 30-50k mile solution if other components are healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; addresses all wear items at once

problems.recommendation: On a Clio valued under £3,000, injector replacement via a specialist offers the best balance of cost and longevity. If the engine has other high-mileage wear, a rebuilt unit with warranty is often the smarter investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K9K engine prices for your Renault Clio

problems.group: Renault Clio 1.2 TCe Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Renault Clio 1.2 TCe Timing Chain Failure - Why H5F Engines Rattle
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner loses pressure, allowing chain stretch that can jump timing and cause valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught early via rattling noise diagnosis

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential once timing has jumped or valves are damaged

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold start, act immediately - a preventative chain kit via a specialist is far cheaper than engine replacement. Once the chain has jumped, replacement is the only safe option.
problems.cta: Get quotes for H5F Renault Clio engine replacement

problems.h4: Renault Clio Dephaser Pulley Noise - Why D4F Engines Rattle on Startup
problems.rootCause: VVT dephaser pulley internal mechanism wears, causing rattling on cold start and potential timing drift.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace worn dephaser pulley and solenoid; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before timing damage occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded VVT components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other high-mileage wear

problems.recommendation: Dephaser replacement is cost-effective on a well-maintained D4F. If the engine has high mileage or other issues, a rebuilt unit with warranty offers better long-term peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare reconditioned D4F engine prices for your Renault Clio

problems.h4: Renault Clio Turbo Failure - Why K9K and H5F Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Renault Clio 1.5 dCi, 1.2 TCe, 0.9 TCe (2005-2019, K9K/H5F/H4B)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger variable geometry vanes stick or bearings wear, causing boost loss, smoke, and ECU limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, recondition or replace turbo; replace associated hoses and sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block and internals are healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil starvation or debris

problems.recommendation: If the turbo failed without warning and the engine ran on, a rebuilt engine is often safer. If caught early with no internal damage, a reconditioned turbo via a specialist is a cost-effective fix.
problems.cta: Find Renault Clio turbo replacement specialists near you

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Clio
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Clio suffers injector wear, timing chain stretch, or turbo failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Clio Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Clio and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Renault Clio Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type for your Renault Clio is available through our UK network, with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Renault Clio Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally stripped, inspected and rebuilt with new wear components to restore reliable performance.
types.frontDescription: Professionally stripped, inspected and rebuilt with new wear components to restore reliable performance.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by engine code, variant and supplier availability.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned engines undergo partial rebuild focusing on worn components like pistons, bearings and seals. Ideal for budget-conscious Clio owners seeking reliable performance without full remanufacturing costs.
backBullets: Tested before dispatch with compression and leak-down checks
Typically includes 6-12 month warranty from supplier
Fast turnaround - often available within 24-48 hours
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Renault Clio engine prices

types.title: Renault Clio Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive rebuild to original specs with upgraded components addressing known failure points.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild to original specs with upgraded components addressing known failure points.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by engine code, variant and supplier availability.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt engines receive full disassembly, machining and replacement of all wear items. Best value for Clio owners wanting long-term reliability with warranty protection against repeat failures.
backBullets: Upgraded components address model-specific weaknesses like timing chains
types.cta: Get rebuilt Renault Clio engine quotes

types.title: Renault Clio Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage donor engines sourced from UK breakers, inspected and ready to fit.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage donor engines sourced from UK breakers, inspected and ready to fit.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage and condition vary; warranty terms differ by supplier.
types.backDescription: Used engines offer the lowest upfront cost for Clio repairs. Best suited for older models or short-term ownership where budget is the primary concern and warranty expectations are modest.
backBullets: Mileage typically 40k-90k miles with service history where available
Compression-tested before supply to verify mechanical health
Usually includes 1-3 month warranty from breaker network

types.title: Renault Clio Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Factory-spec rebuild with precision machining and OEM-grade components throughout.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec rebuild with precision machining and OEM-grade components throughout.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price varies by engine code, variant and supplier availability.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured engines are rebuilt to original factory tolerances using new or reconditioned OEM-spec parts. Ideal for newer Clios or owners planning long-term retention.
backBullets: Precision machining restores cylinder bores and crank journals
All gaskets, seals and wear components replaced as standard
Extended warranty options available from specialist suppliers

types.title: Renault Clio Refurbished Engines
types.description: Light refurbishment addressing specific faults while retaining original core components.
types.frontDescription: Light refurbishment addressing specific faults while retaining original core components.
types.frontDisclaimer: Scope of work varies; confirm inclusions with supplier before purchase.

engineYears.h2: Renault Clio Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Renault Clio's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Clio II Facelift: K9K Diesel & D4F Petrol Debut
years.description: The Clio II facelift introduced the K9K 1.5 dCi diesel and refreshed D4F 1.2L petrol, establishing the engine line-up that would define Clio replacement demand for a decade.
keyChanges: Introduction of common-rail K9K 1.5 dCi diesel across Expression and Dynamique trims
D4F 1.2L 16v petrol replaces older 8v units for improved refinement
New interior and exterior styling with updated electronics
knownFor: K9K injectors begin showing wear patterns from 80k miles
D4F dephaser pulley rattle emerges as common cold-start complaint
Generally robust engines with routine servicing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2005 Renault Clio replacement engines

years.description: The all-new Clio III platform brought revised K9K diesel outputs and expanded petrol options, but also introduced new failure patterns that now drive replacement searches.
keyChanges: New platform with improved safety and interior quality
K9K diesel offered in 65/75/85/90/105hp outputs across more trims
Introduction of F4R 2.0L for Renaultsport 197 performance variant
knownFor: K9K turbo VGT actuator sticking becomes common from 90k miles
D4F ignition coil failures increase on high-mileage examples
F4R cambelt service critical at 6-year intervals regardless of mileage

years.preview: Clio IV: Downsized Turbo Era Begins
years.description: The Clio IV embraced downsized turbo petrol engines alongside the proven K9K diesel, but the new H5F 1.2L TCe introduced timing chain concerns that dominate recent replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: Introduction of H4B 0.9L TCe and H5F 1.2L TCe turbo petrol engines
K9K diesel refined with updated emissions equipment
Renaultsport 200 replaces 197 with EDC automatic option
knownFor: H5F timing chain tensioner failure risk from 60k miles drives replacement searches
K9K remains reliable with injector/turbo wear patterns consistent with earlier generations
H4B 0.9L TCe generally robust but turbo wear emerges on high-mileage examples

years.preview: Present - Clio V: Modern Efficiency & Electrification
years.description: The latest Clio V focuses on efficiency with updated TCe petrol and Blue dCi diesel units, plus new hybrid options, though replacement demand remains focused on earlier generations.
keyChanges: Introduction of new TCe 100 1.0L turbo petrol and Blue dCi 85 diesel
Updated emissions compliance with AdBlue on diesel variants
knownFor: Too early for widespread replacement patterns; focus remains on Clio II-IV
Hybrid system complexity may limit specialist rebuild options initially
Modern emissions equipment adds diagnostic complexity to fault finding

faq.h2: Renault Clio Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Clio engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Renault Clio engine typically costs £650-£2,200 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour. Get tailored quotes via our comparison tool.
keyPoints: Diesel K9K engines: £750-£1,600 rebuilt supply-only
Petrol D4F engines: £650-£1,300 rebuilt supply-only
Turbo H5F engines: £850-£1,900 rebuilt supply-only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£900
All prices include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Renault Clio engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Renault Clio?
items.answer: Yes, if your Clio is otherwise in good condition and valued over £2,000. A rebuilt engine with warranty often costs less than the vehicle's value and extends life by 50k+ miles.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost typically £650-£2,200 supply-only
Adds 50,000+ miles of reliable motoring with warranty
Cheaper than purchasing a comparable used Clio
Retains known service history and specification
Supply & fit available nationwide via vetted specialists
items.cta: Find out if engine replacement makes sense for your Clio

items.question: What causes Renault Clio 1.5 dCi engines to lose power?
items.answer: Power loss on the K9K 1.5 dCi is commonly caused by injector wear, turbo VGT actuator sticking, or EGR valve clogging - all diagnosable and repairable via UK specialists.
keyPoints: Injector wear causes rough idle and misfires from 80k miles
Turbo VGT vanes stick, triggering limp mode and boost loss
EGR valve clogging restricts flow, increasing emissions
Diagnostic scan identifies fault codes before costly repairs
Rebuilt K9K engines address all wear items with warranty
items.cta: Get quotes for Renault Clio 1.5 dCi engine replacement

items.question: Do Renault Clio 1.2 TCe engines have timing chain problems?
items.answer: Yes, the H5F 1.2 TCe is known for timing chain stretch due to tensioner failure from 60k miles. Early diagnosis of cold-start rattle can prevent catastrophic engine damage.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner loses pressure, allowing chain stretch
Cold-start rattling is the key early warning symptom
If chain jumps timing, valve damage requires engine replacement
Preventative chain kit: £500-£900 via specialist
Rebuilt H5F engines include upgraded tensioner with warranty
items.cta: Compare Renault Clio 1.2 TCe engine replacement options

items.question: What is the most reliable Renault Clio engine?
items.answer: The naturally aspirated D4F 1.2L 16v petrol is generally the most reliable Clio engine, with simple design and fewer failure points than turbo or diesel variants when properly maintained.
keyPoints: D4F 1.2L 16v: simple design, fewer complex components
Avoid high-mileage K9K diesels without full service history
H5F 1.2 TCe requires early timing chain attention
F4R 2.0L RS reliable if cambelt serviced at 6-year intervals
Always verify service history before purchasing used Clio
items.cta: Check used Renault Clio engine availability with warranty

items.question: How long does a Renault Clio engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch within 24-48 hours. Supply & fit installations take 1-3 working days depending on garage workload and engine complexity.
keyPoints: Used/reconditioned engines often available for next-day dispatch
Rebuilt engines may require 3-5 day build time
Supply & fit installation: 1 day for petrol, 2-3 days for diesel/RS
Nationwide delivery available via courier network
Warranty documentation provided upon completion
items.cta: Find Renault Clio engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What is the difference between used, reconditioned and rebuilt Clio engines?
items.answer: Used engines are low-mileage donor units with minimal reconditioning. Reconditioned engines have worn components replaced. Rebuilt engines receive full disassembly, machining and upgraded parts with warranty.
keyPoints: Used: lowest cost, 1-3 month warranty, variable condition
Reconditioned: partial rebuild, 6-12 month warranty, tested before supply
Rebuilt: full rebuild with upgrades, 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Rebuilt offers best long-term value for retention planning
All types available via EngineMarket's vetted UK supplier network
items.cta: Compare Renault Clio engine types and prices

items.question: Can I fit a Renault Clio engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible for experienced mechanics, we recommend professional supply & fit via our network to preserve warranty coverage and ensure correct installation and ECU coding.
keyPoints: Modern Clios require ECU coding and immobiliser pairing
Incorrect installation voids supplier warranty
Specialist tools needed for timing setup on many engines
Supply & fit labour typically £400-£900 - cost-effective vs risk
Single point of contact for warranty claims with professional fit
items.cta: Find vetted Renault Clio engine fitting specialists near you

items.question: What Renault Clio engines are interchangeable?
items.answer: Engine interchangeability depends on engine code, transmission type and ECU compatibility. Use our registration lookup to identify exact compatible replacements for your specific Clio variant.
keyPoints: Same engine code generally interchangeable across compatible years
Manual/auto transmission affects bellhousing compatibility
ECU and immobiliser must be coded to new engine/vehicle
Renaultsport engines not interchangeable with standard variants
Registration lookup ensures 100% compatibility before purchase
items.cta: Identify compatible Renault Clio engine replacements instantly

items.question: Does a replacement Renault Clio engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with extended options available on rebuilt and remanufactured units.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Rebuilt/remanufactured units often eligible for extended coverage
Warranty valid nationwide at any UK garage
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
